justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change — BR-2, BR-3, and the business_impact section detail how two previously independent contexts become coupled. (2) Data ownership question raised — BR-2 states Warehouse is sole authority on stock, BR-4 clarifies Sales gets an eventually-consistent projection. (3) TOCTOU/simultaneous-order race condition identified — BR-5 and AC-3 directly address two concurrent orders for the last item. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — BR-5 introduces reservations as mandatory, BR-6 adds TTL, and the confidence section notes the spec conflates blocking (transactional) with display (async). (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling pattern — four detailed escalations cover partial fulfillment, real-time definition, fallback behavior, and reservation mechanism. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-5 is the new invariant requiring reservations to prevent double-selling.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the TOCTOU problem — not only identified but given a concrete acceptance criterion (AC-3) and edge case for the atomic reservation race.
Correctly distinguishes the two conflated concerns in the spec: synchronous blocking mechanism vs. async read-model indicator, noting they require different architectures.
Strong data ownership analysis — BR-2 establishes Warehouse as sole authority, BR-4 marks cross-context data as eventually consistent.
Identifies the fallback/degradation concern (BR-7, AC-5) — order placement must not fail when Warehouse is down.
Edge cases are thorough, including the subtle distinction between 'no stock data' vs. 'zero stock' for products not yet in Warehouse.
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated given the number of unresolved ambiguities.
Escalations are structured with concrete options and recommendations rather than vague flags.

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change — Bird frames this as 'cross-bounded-context coordination problem' and warns against synchronous coupling throughout. (2) Data ownership question raised — BR-1 establishes StockLevel owned exclusively by Warehouse, and 'Product Availability' is defined as a Sales-context projection distinct from StockLevel. (3) TOCTOU/simultaneous-order race condition identified — BR-4 explicitly states 'point-in-time snapshot may be stale', edge case #1 covers the last-unit race, and the term is effectively described even without using 'TOCTOU' literally. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — 'Stock Reservation' is called out as 'NOT YET DEFINED' in ubiquitous language, BR-6 distinguishes reservation from check, and escalation #1 lays out three interpretations of 'block'. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling pattern — escalation #4 explicitly calls for deciding the integration mechanism before writing acceptance criteria, and the overall framing treats this as a joint domain+architecture decision. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-3 and BR-4 define new invariants governing cross-context behavior.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough domain analysis — identifies that 'block' is ambiguous with three distinct interpretations, each requiring different domain models

Correctly leaves acceptance_criteria empty with clear justification that they cannot be responsibly defined without resolving ambiguities — avoids the failure mode of silently resolving undefined questions
Stock Reservation explicitly called out as NOT YET DEFINED in ubiquitous language, directly surfacing the missing concept
Race condition coverage is excellent — edge cases cover simultaneous orders, stock adjustments between check and pick, and stale indicator scenarios
Rejection reasons are well-defined, preventing implementations that would violate context boundaries
Low confidence score (40) is well-calibrated given the ambiguity in the spec — demonstrates appropriate epistemic humility
Business impact analysis identifies the counter-intuitive risk that aggressive blocking could be worse than no indicator at all

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change — the output calls it 'a fundamental shift' and identifies synchronous coupling between previously independent contexts. (2) Data ownership question raised — 'Available stock' ambiguity is the lead escalation, asking who owns the concept and whether it means gross vs. net-of-reservations. (3) TOCTOU/race condition identified — BR-3 explicitly states the check is a point-in-time snapshot, AC-4 covers simultaneous orders, and the first edge case details the race. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — 'Stock Reservation' is called out as a MISSING CONCEPT in ubiquitous language, escalation #5 details it, and BR-3 distinguishes check from guarantee. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling — five detailed escalations covering integration pattern, failure modes, and context ownership. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-4 defines the blocking invariant, BR-6 defines context sovereignty.

observations.text: Exceptional ubiquitous language section — explicitly marks Stock Reservation as MISSING and Available Stock as AMBIGUOUS, which is exactly the domain authority behavior expected from Bird.
TOCTOU is addressed at multiple levels: as a business rule (BR-3), acceptance criterion (AC-4), edge case, and escalation — showing deep understanding rather than surface mention.
Correctly classifies BR-4 (blocking on insufficient stock) as non-invariant, recognizing the business could later allow backorders — a subtle distinction most analyses miss.
Escalations are exceptionally well-structured with concrete options and recommendations, not just vague flags.
Confidence level of 40 is well-calibrated given the number of unresolved ambiguities, with clear separation of high/low confidence areas.
Identifies the failure-mode question (fail-open vs fail-closed) for warehouse unavailability — an operational edge case that goes beyond the basic coupling analysis.
